How Moodle capabilities control access to Paper Entry.

import { Aside } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

Paper Entry uses three Moodle capabilities to control access. All capabilities
live under the `quiz/paperentry` namespace.

---

## Capability reference

### `quiz/paperentry:view`

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| **Archetypes** | `teacher`, `editingteacher`, `manager` |
| **Risk bitmask** | `RISK_PERSONAL` |
| **Description** | View the Paper Entry report tab and download the grading sheet. |

This is the minimum capability needed to see the tab at all. It is granted
to all standard teaching roles by default.

---

### `quiz/paperentry:submit`

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| **Archetypes** | *(none)* |
| **Risk bitmask** | `RISK_SPAM` |
| **Description** | Upload a filled grader CSV for a specific quiz. |

This capability is **not** granted to any role archetype. It is assigned
per-quiz by the manager, at the **module context**, to specific users via
the internal `paperentry_grader` role.

When a manager adds a user as a grader, the following happens automatically:
1. The `paperentry_grader` role is created (if it doesn't exist).
2. The user is assigned that role at the quiz's module context.
3. The role grants `quiz/paperentry:submit` only at `CONTEXT_MODULE` level.

When a grader is removed, the role assignment is revoked.

<Aside type="note">
  Because the capability is scoped to a single module context, a grader for
  Quiz A cannot submit answers for Quiz B, even within the same course.
</Aside>

---

### `quiz/paperentry:manage`

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| **Archetypes** | `manager` |
| **Risk bitmask** | `RISK_PERSONAL`, `RISK_DATALOSS` |
| **Description** | Full control: configure settings, assign graders, view comparison table, trigger gradebook import. |

This capability is restricted to the **Manager** role archetype. Regular editing
teachers do **not** have this capability by default — they can view and download
but cannot trigger the final import.

<Aside type="caution">
  Site administrators bypass all capability checks in Moodle and always have
  full access. If you are testing role behaviour, use a non-admin account.
</Aside>

---

## Role architecture

```
Site Administrator
 └── bypasses all checks (Moodle core behaviour)

Manager (course role)
 └── quiz/paperentry:view    ✓
 └── quiz/paperentry:manage  ✓
 └── quiz/paperentry:submit  ✗ (not needed — manage covers everything)

Editing Teacher (course role)
 └── quiz/paperentry:view    ✓
 └── quiz/paperentry:manage  ✗
 └── quiz/paperentry:submit  ✗

paperentry_grader (module-level internal role)
 └── quiz/paperentry:submit  ✓ (only at the specific quiz's module context)
```

---

## Customising capabilities

You can grant additional capabilities to roles through **Site administration →
Users → Permissions → Define roles**, or at the course/activity level through
the standard Moodle override interface.

Common customisations:

| Goal | Action |
|---|---|
| Allow editing teachers to manage imports | Grant `quiz/paperentry:manage` to `editingteacher` archetype |
| Create a dedicated paper-exam coordinator role | Create a custom role with `view` + `manage`; assign to coordinators |
| Allow a student to be a grader | Assign the `paperentry_grader` role to the student at module context, or use the *Add grader* UI |
Configuring which student columns appear in the grading sheet.

import { Aside } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

Export settings control what *student identification* columns are included in the
grading sheet alongside the answer columns.

Settings are **per-quiz** — different quizzes in the same course can have different
export configurations.

---

## Opening the settings

Open the quiz → Paper Entry tab (requires `quiz/paperentry:manage`). The
**Export Settings** card is at the top of the page.

---

## Available columns

The tag-picker UI shows all profile fields that are available on the site:

| Field | Description |
|---|---|
| `email` | The student's Moodle account email address |
| `idnumber` | Institutional / student ID number |
| `firstname` | First name |
| `lastname` | Last name |
| `phone1` | Primary phone |
| `phone2` | Secondary phone |
| `department` | Department |
| `institution` | Institution |
| `city` | City / town |
| `country` | Country code |
| `profile_field_*` | Any custom profile fields defined on the site |

Only fields that have values for at least some enrolled students are useful to
include.

---

## How the student is identified at import

During import, the plugin uses **the first recognised profile column** it finds in
the uploaded CSV to look up the student's Moodle account.

Priority order:
1. `idnumber` — preferred because it is stable and unique
2. `email` — reliable but can change
3. `username` — if present
4. `firstname` + `lastname` combined — last resort; ambiguous for students with the
   same name

<Aside type="tip">
  Always include **`idnumber`** if your institution assigns stable student ID numbers.
  It is the most reliable identifier and reduces the chance of import errors caused
  by name changes or shared email domains.
</Aside>

---

## Saving settings

Click **Save settings** after making changes. The settings are stored in
`quiz_paperentry_settings` and take effect immediately on the next CSV export.

---

## Re-exporting after settings change

If you change the export settings after graders have already downloaded the blank
sheet, you will need to:

1. Re-export the grading sheet.
2. Notify graders to use the new template (old uploads with different column
   structures may fail validation).

<Aside type="caution">
  If a grader uploads a CSV that is missing the profile column the plugin expects,
  the import will fail with an error. Always share the updated grading sheet when you
  change export settings.
</Aside>
Map short grader notations to full Moodle answer texts.

import { Aside } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

**Value Substitutions** let a manager define shorthand mappings so graders can enter
brief codes in their CSV instead of typing full answer texts.

---

## Why they are needed

Moodle multichoice question options often have long text labels:

```
Option A: "The process of converting sunlight into chemical energy"
Option B: "The release of energy from glucose in the presence of oxygen"
Option C: "The transport of water from roots to leaves via the xylem"
```

Asking graders to type these in full is error-prone. With value substitutions you
can allow graders to enter `a`, `b`, or `c` instead.

---

## Defining substitutions

Open the quiz → Paper Entry tab → **Value Substitutions** card.

Each substitution is a pair:

| Field | Description |
|---|---|
| **From** | What the grader will type in the CSV |
| **To** | The full Moodle answer text it maps to |

### Example mappings

| From | To | Use case |
|---|---|---|
| `a` | `Paris` | Letter-to-option mapping for multichoice |
| `1` | `Paris` | Number-to-option mapping |
| `T` | `True` | Short true/false |
| `F` | `False` | Short true/false |
| `Y` | `Yes` | Yes/No questions |

<Aside type="tip">
  Substitutions are applied **before** answer validation. A grader who types `T`
  will have it silently converted to `True` before the import checks whether `True`
  is a valid answer.
</Aside>

---

## Scope

Substitutions are **per-quiz** — they apply to all questions in the quiz and to all
grader uploads for that quiz.

They are **not** question-specific. If you map `a → Paris` but another question has
an option `Amsterdam`, a grader cannot use `a` for both — only the first match wins.

For quizzes where different questions need different mappings, use distinctive codes
(`q1a`, `q2a`, etc.) or instruct graders to write full answer texts for ambiguous
questions.

---

## Managing substitutions

### Adding a mapping

Click **Add row** in the Value Substitutions card, fill in the *From* and *To*
fields, and click **Save**.

### Editing a mapping

Click the edit icon next to any row, change the values, and click **Save**.

### Removing a mapping

Click the delete icon next to the row you want to remove.

### Mapping widget

The substitution UI uses a drag-and-drop tag-picker widget that also supports bulk
import — you can paste a comma-separated list of `from:to` pairs into the text area.

---

## How substitutions interact with validation

During the import two-pass validation:

1. Each CSV cell value is looked up in the substitution table (case-insensitive).
2. If a match is found, the value is replaced with the mapped text.
3. The mapped text is then validated against the question's answer options.

If the mapped text does **not** match any option, the import is aborted with an error
that shows both the original CSV value *and* the valid options:

```
Row 3: "x" does not match any answer option for question "Capital cities".
Valid options are: "Paris", "London", "Berlin", "Madrid".
```

<Aside type="note">
  The error shows the **original CSV value** (`x`), not the mapped value, so graders
  can easily trace the issue back to their spreadsheet.
</Aside>
How to install Paper Entry on your Moodle 5.0+ site.

import { Steps, Aside } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

## Requirements

| Requirement | Version |
|---|---|
| Moodle | **5.0 or later** |

Paper Entry requires Moodle 5.0+. PHP version and database requirements are
determined by your Moodle version — no additional server requirements beyond
what Moodle itself needs.

---

## Installing from a ZIP

<Steps>

1. Download the latest release ZIP from the
   [GitHub Releases](https://github.com/mussaab/moodle-quiz-paperentry/releases) page
   or from the [Moodle Plugin Directory](https://moodle.org/plugins/).

2. Log in to your Moodle site as a **site administrator**.

3. Go to **Site administration → Plugins → Install plugins**.

4. Drag the ZIP into the upload area (or use the file picker) and click
   **Install plugin from the ZIP file**.

5. Moodle will validate the package and show a confirmation screen. Click
   **Continue**.

6. Review and confirm the database upgrade on the **Notifications** page.

</Steps>

---

## Installing manually (Git / SSH)

<Steps>

1. SSH into your server and `cd` to your Moodle root.

2. Clone (or copy) the plugin into the correct location:

   ```bash
   git clone https://github.com/mussaab/moodle-quiz-paperentry.git \
       mod/quiz/report/paperentry
   ```

3. Fix ownership so your web server can read the files:

   ```bash
   chown -R www-data:www-data mod/quiz/report/paperentry
   ```

4. Log in as a site administrator and navigate to
   **Site administration → Notifications** to run the database upgrade.

</Steps>

---

## What the installer creates

The upgrade script creates four database tables:

| Table | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `quiz_paperentry_settings` | Per-quiz export configuration (extra profile columns) |
| `quiz_paperentry_graders` | Grader assignments per quiz |
| `quiz_paperentry_attempts` | Which Moodle quiz attempts were created by this plugin |
| `quiz_paperentry_mappings` | Per-quiz value-substitution rules |

All student-facing data (grades, attempt details) lives in standard Moodle tables
(`quiz_attempts`, `question_attempts`, etc.) and is managed by Moodle core.

---

## Upgrading

Re-run the installer steps with the new ZIP (or `git pull` on your clone) and visit
**Site administration → Notifications**. The upgrade script is idempotent — it will
only apply steps that have not been applied yet.

<Aside type="note">
  After upgrading from a version that stored grader CSV submissions in the database
  (`csvdata` column), the upgrade script automatically migrates all existing
  submissions to the Moodle File API and drops the old column. This is irreversible —
  take a full database and `moodledata` backup before upgrading.
</Aside>

---

## Uninstalling

Go to **Site administration → Plugins → Plugins overview**, find *Paper Entry* under
*Quiz reports*, and click **Uninstall**. Moodle will drop all plugin tables and
remove all plugin files from `moodledata`.

<Aside type="caution">
  Uninstalling removes grader assignments and value mappings. Student quiz attempts
  that were already created by the plugin are **not** deleted — they are standard
  Moodle quiz attempts and will remain in the gradebook.
</Aside>
What Paper Entry is, its two workflows, and why it exists.

import { Aside } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

**Paper Entry** is a [Moodle](https://moodle.org) quiz report plugin that bridges
the gap between paper-based exams and Moodle's gradebook.

Instead of typing every student's answers manually after a paper exam, a manager
(or an assigned grader) exports a grading sheet, fills it in with student
answers from the collected papers, then imports it — Moodle automatically grades
each student's responses exactly as it would a standard online quiz attempt.

---

## Two import workflows

Paper Entry offers two distinct approaches. Choose based on your marking setup.

### Workflow A — Direct import

The simplest path. The manager fills in the grading sheet with student answers and
imports it directly — no other teachers are involved. Moodle grades the attempts
automatically using the standard quiz engine.

```
Manager fills grading sheet → uploads via Direct Import → Moodle grades automatically
```

**When to use:**
- Small classes where one teacher handles everything
- Single-grader setups
- Emergency re-imports after a mistake

The *Direct Import — Admin Override* section on the manager dashboard handles this
workflow. It is collapsed by default to keep the interface clean.

---

### Workflow B — Multi-grader with comparison

Two or more teachers each independently fill in a grading sheet and upload it.
The plugin compares their answers side-by-side. Graders can see where their answers
conflict (without seeing what others entered) and re-upload until everything agrees.
Only then can the manager submit to the gradebook.

```
Grader 1 uploads → sees own conflicts → fixes → re-uploads ──┐
                                                               ├─→ Manager submits → Moodle grades
Grader 2 uploads → sees own conflicts → fixes → re-uploads ──┘
```

**When to use:**
- Formal end-of-term or standardised exams
- Quality-assurance double-marking
- Moderated assessments where two independent marks are required

<Aside type="tip">
  Workflow B is the recommended approach for formal exams because graders catch and
  fix their own disagreements independently — before grades are committed.
</Aside>

---

## Who is it for?

| Role | Workflow A | Workflow B |
|---|---|---|
| **Manager / Course coordinator** | Fills grading sheet, imports directly | Assigns graders, monitors comparison table, submits to gradebook |
| **Teacher / Grader** | — | Downloads grading sheet, fills it in, uploads, reviews own conflicts, re-uploads until green |
| **Student** | Sits the paper exam; Moodle shows grade automatically | Same |

---

## Supported question types

Paper Entry works with **`multichoice`** and **`truefalse`** questions only.

<Aside type="caution">
  Questions with "Shuffle answers" enabled will block the grading sheet export — the
  plugin cannot guarantee a stable answer mapping when answer order is randomised.
</Aside>

---

## How it fits into Moodle

Paper Entry is a **quiz report subplugin** (`mod/quiz/report/paperentry`). It lives
entirely inside Moodle — no external service, no separate database, no cron job
required. Student attempts it creates are standard Moodle quiz attempts — graded by
Moodle's own quiz engine, indistinguishable from attempts made directly online.

All database access uses the Moodle DBAL (`$DB` API) with no vendor-specific SQL —
compatible with MySQL, MariaDB, and PostgreSQL.

All JavaScript is delivered through Moodle's AMD module system; all HTML is rendered
through Mustache templates — fully compatible with Moodle's Content Security Policy.

---

## Next step

Head to [Installation](/getting-started/installation/).
Set up and run your first paper exam import in under 10 minutes.

import { Steps, Aside, Tabs, TabItem } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

Paper Entry supports two workflows. Pick the one that fits your setup.

<Tabs>
<TabItem label="Workflow A — Direct import">

The manager grades the papers and imports directly. No other teachers involved.

**Before you begin:** you need a quiz with at least one `multichoice` or `truefalse`
question, with "Shuffle answers" off. You need `quiz/paperentry:manage`.

<Steps>

1. Open the quiz → **Paper Entry** tab.

2. *(Optional)* In **Export Settings**, tick any extra profile columns (ID number, class, etc.) and click **Save settings**.

3. Click **Download grading sheet**. Students sit their paper exam as normal. Afterwards, fill in the grading sheet with each student's answers from their paper sheet.

4. Scroll to **Direct Import — Admin Override** (collapsed by default), expand it, choose your filled CSV, and click **Import**.

5. Grades appear in the quiz gradebook immediately.

</Steps>

<Aside type="tip">
  Re-importing replaces previous Paper Entry attempts — it won't create duplicates.
</Aside>

</TabItem>
<TabItem label="Workflow B — Multi-grader">

Two or more graders mark independently. A comparison table catches every disagreement before grades are committed.

**Before you begin:** same as Workflow A, plus at least two teachers who will grade independently.

<Steps>

1. Open the quiz → **Paper Entry** tab.

2. In **Export Settings**, choose extra profile columns and click **Save settings**.

3. In the **Graders** card, add each independent grader by name.

4. Students sit their paper exam as normal. Each grader then downloads the grading sheet themselves (via the **Download grading sheet** button on their Paper Entry tab view) and fills in student answers from the collected paper sheets independently.

5. Each grader opens the quiz → Paper Entry tab, uploads their filled CSV via **Submit Your Answers**, and clicks **Submit**.

6. Graders see their own conflict status (colour-coded, without seeing other graders' answers) and re-upload until all cells turn green. As manager you monitor the full comparison table with all graders' answers side-by-side.

7. When every cell is **green** (all graders agree), click **Submit to Gradebook**.

8. Graded quiz attempts are created for all students — grades appear immediately.

</Steps>

<Aside type="tip">
  Use [Value Substitutions](/configuration/value-substitutions/) to let graders enter
  `T`/`F` or `1`/`2`/`3` instead of full answer text.
</Aside>

</TabItem>
</Tabs>
Paper-based quiz grading workflow for Moodle 5.0+

**Paper Entry** is a Moodle quiz report plugin that bridges the gap between paper exams and Moodle's gradebook. Students sit a regular paper exam. Afterwards, a manager (or assigned graders) exports a grading sheet, fills it in with the students' answers from the collected papers, and imports it to automatically create graded quiz attempts.

👉 [Get Started](/getting-started/introduction/) — [Quick Start](/getting-started/quick-start/)

---

## Two workflows

Paper Entry supports two distinct import workflows. Choose the one that fits your context.

### Workflow A — Direct import

The manager grades the papers and imports the results without involving other teachers.

| Step | Who | Action |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Manager | Export grading sheet |
| 2 | Manager | Fill in student answers |
| 3 | Manager | Upload via **Direct Import** → committed to gradebook immediately |

Best for: small classes, single-teacher setups, emergency re-grades.

---

### Workflow B — Multi-grader with comparison *(recommended for formal exams)*

Two or more teachers grade independently. Every disagreement is highlighted before anything is committed.

| Step | Who | Action |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Manager | Configure export columns, assign graders |
| 2 | Students | Sit their regular paper exam |
| 3 | Each grader | Download grading sheet, fill in student answers from papers, upload via **Submit Your Answers** |
| 4 | Each grader | See their own conflict status (without seeing other graders' answers) — re-upload until all cells are green |
| 5 | Manager | Monitor the full comparison table (all graders' answers side-by-side) — submit to gradebook once all agree |

Best for: formal exams, quality-assurance double-marking, moderated assessments.

---

## Key features

- **Two import workflows** — direct manager import or independent multi-grader comparison
- **Colour-coded comparison table** — instantly spot every disagreement between graders
- **Two-pass validation** — invalid answers abort the entire import cleanly; nothing is half-written
- **Value substitutions** — let graders use shorthand (`T`, `F`, `1`, `2`, `3`) instead of full answer text
- **Edit Answer** — correct a single student's answer in-place without re-uploading
- **GDPR compliant** — full Moodle Privacy API support for students and graders
- **Cross-DB compatible** — all queries use the Moodle DBAL; no vendor-specific SQL
- **CSP compatible** — all JavaScript via AMD modules, all HTML via Mustache templates

---

## Requirements

| Requirement | Version |
|---|---|
| Moodle | **5.0 or later** |
| Questions | `multichoice` and `truefalse` only |

Version history and release notes for Paper Entry.

All notable changes to this project are documented here.
The format follows [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.0.0/).

---

## [1.0.0] — 2026-06-16

Initial public release.

### Features

- **Blank CSV export** with configurable student profile columns (email, ID number,
  custom profile fields) selected via a tag-picker UI.
- **Multi-grader workflow** — managers assign independent graders; each grader
  uploads their own filled sheet.
- **Grader comparison table** — colour-coded per-student, per-question view
  highlighting every disagreement between graders.
- **Gated gradebook import** — the Submit button is enabled only when all graders
  have uploaded and every answer agrees.
- **Two-pass import validation** — all rows are validated before anything is written
  to the database; a single bad answer aborts the entire import cleanly.
- **Inline valid-options in error messages** — invalid answer errors list all valid
  options directly in the message.
- **Value substitutions** — managers define shorthand mappings (e.g. `T → True`,
  `1 → Paris`) so graders can use any notation they prefer.
- **Edit Answer** — managers correct a single student's answer in-place without
  re-uploading a full CSV.
- **Shuffle-answer detection** — the export button is disabled if any question has
  "Shuffle answers" turned on.
- **Direct Import override** — managers can bypass the grader workflow and import a
  CSV immediately (collapsed by default).
- **Moodle Component Library compliance** — all JavaScript via AMD modules; all HTML
  via Mustache templates; fully CSP-compatible.
- **File API storage** — grader CSV submissions stored via the Moodle File API
  (not raw database text).
- **Privacy API** — full GDPR support: metadata declaration, data export, and
  deletion for both students and graders.
- **Capability system** — three capabilities (`view`, `submit`, `manage`) with a
  dedicated `paperentry_grader` role scoped to the module context.
- **Database integrity** — foreign keys and indexes on all plugin tables.
- **PHPUnit test suite** — 15 tests covering import, export, and privacy logic.
- **Supported Moodle versions** — 5.0 and 5.1.

All notable changes to this project are documented here. The format follows Keep a Changelog.

Features

Section titled “Features”
  • Blank CSV export with configurable student profile columns (email, ID number, custom profile fields) selected via a tag-picker UI.
  • Multi-grader workflow — managers assign independent graders; each grader uploads their own filled sheet.
  • Grader comparison table — colour-coded per-student, per-question view highlighting every disagreement between graders.
  • Gated gradebook import — the Submit button is enabled only when all graders have uploaded and every answer agrees.
  • Two-pass import validation — all rows are validated before anything is written to the database; a single bad answer aborts the entire import cleanly.
  • Inline valid-options in error messages — invalid answer errors list all valid options directly in the message.
  • Value substitutions — managers define shorthand mappings (e.g. T → True, 1 → Paris) so graders can use any notation they prefer.
  • Edit Answer — managers correct a single student’s answer in-place without re-uploading a full CSV.
  • Shuffle-answer detection — the export button is disabled if any question has “Shuffle answers” turned on.
  • Direct Import override — managers can bypass the grader workflow and import a CSV immediately (collapsed by default).
  • Moodle Component Library compliance — all JavaScript via AMD modules; all HTML via Mustache templates; fully CSP-compatible.
  • File API storage — grader CSV submissions stored via the Moodle File API (not raw database text).
  • Privacy API — full GDPR support: metadata declaration, data export, and deletion for both students and graders.
  • Capability system — three capabilities (view, submit, manage) with a dedicated paperentry_grader role scoped to the module context.
  • Database integrity — foreign keys and indexes on all plugin tables.
  • PHPUnit test suite — 15 tests covering import, export, and privacy logic.
  • Supported Moodle versions — 5.0 and 5.1.
How to contribute to Paper Entry development.

import { Steps, Aside } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

Contributions are welcome — bug reports, feature suggestions, documentation
improvements, and code changes are all appreciated.

---

## Getting started

<Steps>

1. **Fork** the repository on GitHub.

2. **Clone** your fork:
   ```bash
   git clone https://github.com/YOUR-USERNAME/moodle-quiz-paperentry.git
   cd moodle-quiz-paperentry
   ```

3. **Link** the plugin into a Moodle development instance:
   ```bash
   ln -s $(pwd) /path/to/moodle/mod/quiz/report/paperentry
   ```

4. **Create a branch** for your change:
   ```bash
   git checkout -b feature/my-improvement
   ```

5. Make your changes, add tests, and verify everything passes.

6. **Push** and open a pull request against `main`.

</Steps>

---

## Development environment

You will need:

- Moodle 5.0 or 5.1 development instance (Docker or MAMP both work).
- PHP 8.1+ with Xdebug for test coverage.
- Node.js 18+ for AMD build (`grunt amd`).
- Composer for PHPUnit.

### AMD build

After editing `amd/src/*.js`, rebuild the minified AMD output:

```bash
# From Moodle root
grunt amd --root=mod/quiz/report/paperentry
```

This writes the minified files to `amd/build/`.

<Aside type="note">
  Never commit unminified AMD source changes without also committing the
  corresponding `amd/build/` file. Moodle serves the build file in production.
</Aside>

---

## Code standards

### PHP

Follow the [Moodle coding style](https://moodledev.io/general/development/policies/codingstyle).
Run the checker before every commit:

```bash
php local/codechecker/cli/check.php mod/quiz/report/paperentry
```

Key rules:
- 4-space indentation (no tabs).
- PHP file header with `@package`, `@copyright`, `@license` docblock.
- All output via `$OUTPUT` or Mustache templates — no raw `echo`.
- No inline `<script>` — use `$PAGE->requires->js_call_amd()`.

### JavaScript (AMD)

- ES2017 syntax is fine (Moodle 5.x supports it).
- Use `core/ajax`, `core/str`, `core/notification` from the Moodle AMD library
  where available — don't reinvent the wheel.
- Keep modules small and single-purpose.

### Mustache templates

- No PHP logic in templates — compute all data in PHP and pass a flat context.
- Use `{{# str }} string_key, plugin_component {{/ str }}` for localised strings.
- Run `vendor/bin/phpunit --filter mustache` to validate template syntax.

---

## Tests

Add PHPUnit tests for any new functionality. Tests live in `tests/`:

```bash
# Run the full suite
vendor/bin/phpunit mod/quiz/report/paperentry/tests/

# Run with coverage
vendor/bin/phpunit --coverage-html /tmp/cov \
    mod/quiz/report/paperentry/tests/
```

Target: every new public method should have at least one test.

---

## Pull request checklist

- [ ] Code passes `phpcs --standard=moodle`
- [ ] AMD build files updated (`grunt amd`)
- [ ] PHPUnit tests pass
- [ ] New functionality has test coverage
- [ ] Language strings added to `lang/en/quiz_paperentry.php`
- [ ] `version.php` bumped with a new `$plugin->version` timestamp
- [ ] CHANGELOG entry added under a new `[x.y.z]` heading
- [ ] PR description explains *why* the change is needed, not just *what* it does

---

## Reporting bugs

Open a [GitHub Issue](https://github.com/mussaab/moodle-quiz-paperentry/issues)
with:

1. Moodle version and Paper Entry version.
2. Steps to reproduce.
3. Expected behaviour vs. actual behaviour.
4. Any relevant error messages from the Moodle logs.

Technical overview of how Paper Entry is structured.

## Plugin type

Paper Entry is a **quiz report subplugin** of type `quiz` (not `mod`). Its
component name is `quiz_paperentry` and it lives at:

```
mod/quiz/report/paperentry/
```

Moodle discovers it automatically via the `mod/quiz/report/` scan in the quiz
module bootstrap.

---

## Directory structure

```
paperentry/
├── amd/
│   └── src/
│       ├── edit_answer.js          # AMD module — in-place answer editing
│       ├── mapping_widget.js       # AMD module — value-substitution tag picker
│       └── export_settings.js      # AMD module — export column tag picker
├── classes/
│   ├── export.php                  # CSV export: grading sheet generation
│   ├── import.php                  # CSV import: two-pass validation + DB commit
│   ├── settings_manager.php        # Per-quiz settings, grader management, File API
│   └── privacy/
│       └── provider.php            # Moodle Privacy API implementation
├── db/
│   ├── access.php                  # Capability definitions
│   ├── install.xml                 # XMLDB schema (tables, keys, indexes)
│   └── upgrade.php                 # Upgrade steps
├── lang/
│   └── en/
│       └── quiz_paperentry.php     # English language strings
├── templates/
│   ├── shuffle_warning.mustache
│   ├── question_reference.mustache
│   └── import_results.mustache
├── tests/
│   ├── import_test.php             # PHPUnit — import logic
│   ├── export_test.php             # PHPUnit — export logic
│   └── privacy/
│       └── provider_test.php       # PHPUnit — Privacy API
├── report.php                      # Main entry point — renders all UI sections
└── version.php                     # Plugin metadata
```

---

## Request lifecycle

```
mod/quiz/report.php
  └── instantiates quiz_paperentry\report (report.php)
        ├── checks capabilities
        ├── handles form actions (POST: gradersubmit, finalimport, etc.)
        └── renders view sections (GET)
              ├── $OUTPUT->render_from_template(...)   ← Mustache for static HTML
              └── $PAGE->requires->js_call_amd(...)    ← AMD for interactive widgets
```

All actions are gated by `confirm_sesskey()` (CSRF protection).

---

## Class responsibilities

### `export.php` — `quiz_paperentry\export`

- `get_questions(int $quizid): array` — returns all multichoice/truefalse questions
  in quiz slot order; appends `_slot{n}` suffix to duplicate names.
- `get_enrolled_students(int $courseid): array` — returns enrolled users with
  student role (excludes teachers and managers).
- `generate_csv(int $quizid, array $extrafields): string` — builds the grading sheet
  string.
- `csv_safe(string $value): string` — prefixes formula-injection characters.

### `import.php` — `quiz_paperentry\import`

- `process(string $csvpath, int $quizid): bool` — two-pass import entry point.
  Returns `true` on full success, `false` if any validation error occurred.
- `validate_row(array $row, int $rownum): ?array` — validates one CSV row; returns
  `['userid' => int, 'answers' => array]` or `null` (skip row) or appends to
  `$this->errors`.
- `create_attempt(int $userid, array $answers): void` — commits a single student's
  attempt using the Moodle quiz engine API.
- `get_errors(): array`, `get_warnings(): array`, `get_imported(): int` — result
  accessors used by the rendering layer.

### `settings_manager.php` — `quiz_paperentry\settings_manager`

- Quiz settings CRUD (`get_settings`, `save_settings`).
- Grader management (`add_grader`, `remove_grader`, `get_graders`).
- File API bridge (`save_submission`, `get_submission_file`,
  `get_all_submissions_csv`).
- Role management (`get_or_create_grader_role`).

### `privacy/provider.php`

Implements:
- `core_privacy\local\metadata\provider`
- `core_privacy\local\request\plugin\provider`
- `core_privacy\local\request\core_userlist_provider`

---

## AMD modules

All JavaScript is loaded via `$PAGE->requires->js_call_amd()` — never inline.
The three AMD modules are in `amd/src/`:

| Module | `init()` signature | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `quiz_paperentry/mapping_widget` | `(widgetid, fromlabel, tolabel, injectintoid)` | Value-substitution tag-picker |
| `quiz_paperentry/edit_answer` | `(qdata, currentanswers, noattemptstr, currentstr)` | In-place answer editor |
| `quiz_paperentry/export_settings` | `(widgetid, availablefields, selectedfields)` | Export-column tag-picker |

AMD build output goes to `amd/build/` (generated by `grunt amd`).

---

## File API storage

Grader CSV submissions are stored in the Moodle File API under:

| Key | Value |
|---|---|
| `component` | `quiz_paperentry` |
| `filearea` | `submission` |
| `contextid` | Module context id of the quiz |
| `itemid` | Moodle user id of the grader |
| `filepath` | `/` |
| `filename` | `submission.csv` |

This means files are:
- Governed by Moodle's file permissions system.
- Included in Moodle's GDPR data export/deletion for the grader.
- Backed up if the Moodle backup includes the module context files (note:
  quiz report plugins are not currently called during backup — see
  [Backup & Restore](/technical/backup-restore/)).

Section titled “File API storage”

Grader CSV submissions are stored in the Moodle File API under:

KeyValue

fileareasubmission

This means files are:

  • Governed by Moodle’s file permissions system.

What is and isn't backed up when you back up a Moodle quiz.

import { Aside } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

<Aside type="caution" title="Known architectural limitation">
  Moodle's quiz backup engine only calls **`quizaccess`** subplugins during backup.
  Quiz **report** subplugins (including Paper Entry) are not invoked. This is a gap
  in Moodle core that cannot be worked around without patching core files.
</Aside>

---

## What IS backed up

Moodle's standard quiz backup includes everything in core quiz tables:

| Data | Backed up? |
|---|---|
| Quiz structure (questions, slots, grades) | ✅ Yes |
| Student quiz attempts and grades | ✅ Yes |
| Question attempt details (per-question responses) | ✅ Yes |

Quiz attempts created by Paper Entry are **standard Moodle quiz attempts** stored
in `quiz_attempts`, `question_attempts`, etc. They are fully included in every
quiz backup and restore correctly without any special handling.

---

## What is NOT backed up

| Data | Backed up? | Why |
|---|---|---|
| Grader assignments (`quiz_paperentry_graders`) | ❌ No | Backup API not called |
| Export settings (`quiz_paperentry_settings`) | ❌ No | Backup API not called |
| Value substitutions (`quiz_paperentry_mappings`) | ❌ No | Backup API not called |
| Grader CSV submissions (File API) | ❌ No | Backup API not called |

---

## Impact of a restore

After restoring a quiz backup:

1. **Student grades are intact** — all quiz attempts restore correctly.
2. **Graders must be re-assigned** — the grader list is empty after restore.
3. **Export settings must be reconfigured** — extra profile columns are lost.
4. **Value substitutions must be re-entered** — mapping rules are lost.

For most use cases, re-configuring takes only a few minutes.

---

## Workaround: manual data export

If you need to preserve Paper Entry configuration across a site migration, you can
manually export the relevant tables before migration and import them after:

```sql
-- Export (run on source site)
SELECT * FROM mdl_quiz_paperentry_settings WHERE quizid = ?;
SELECT * FROM mdl_quiz_paperentry_graders  WHERE quizid = ?;
SELECT * FROM mdl_quiz_paperentry_mappings WHERE quizid = ?;
```

After restoring, insert the rows with updated `quizid` values (the quiz ID changes
on restore).

---

## Roadmap

A Moodle tracker issue should be raised to add quiz report subplugin backup support
to Moodle core. Once core supports it, Paper Entry will implement the backup/restore
classes:

- `backup_quiz_paperentry_stepslib.php` — `backup_quiz_paperentry_activity_structure_step`
- `restore_quiz_paperentry_stepslib.php` — `restore_quiz_paperentry_activity_structure_step`

If you need this feature urgently, consider contributing it to both Moodle core
(the quiz backup framework) and this plugin.

---

## Course reset

When a teacher resets a course (removing student data), Paper Entry responds to the
reset form via the standard Moodle reset mechanism. The reset removes:

- `quiz_paperentry_attempts` records for the quiz (the tracking link between
  Paper Entry and the Moodle attempt is removed).
- The Moodle quiz attempts themselves are removed by Moodle core's quiz reset handler.

Grader assignments and export settings are **not** removed by a course reset —
they are configuration, not student data.
Table definitions, relationships, and indexes for Paper Entry.

import { Aside } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

Paper Entry creates four tables in the Moodle database. All tables are defined in
`db/install.xml` using XMLDB and follow Moodle naming conventions
(`quiz_paperentry_*`).

---

## `quiz_paperentry_settings`

Stores per-quiz export configuration.

| Column | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `id` | `BIGINT` | Primary key (auto-increment) |
| `quizid` | `BIGINT` | FK → `quiz.id` |
| `extrafields` | `TEXT` | JSON-encoded array of profile field shortnames |
| `timecreated` | `BIGINT` | Unix timestamp |
| `timemodified` | `BIGINT` | Unix timestamp |

**Indexes:** `quizid`

---

## `quiz_paperentry_graders`

Records which users are assigned as graders for a given quiz.

| Column | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `id` | `BIGINT` | Primary key |
| `quizid` | `BIGINT` | FK → `quiz.id` |
| `userid` | `BIGINT` | FK → `user.id` |
| `timecreated` | `BIGINT` | When the grader was assigned |

**Indexes:** `quizid`, `userid`

<Aside type="note">
  The `paperentry_grader` role assignment (which grants `quiz/paperentry:submit`)
  is managed separately in `mdl_role_assignments` — this table only records the
  *intent* of the grader assignment for display and lookup purposes.
</Aside>

---

## `quiz_paperentry_attempts`

Links Paper Entry imports to the Moodle quiz attempts they created.

| Column | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `id` | `BIGINT` | Primary key |
| `quizid` | `BIGINT` | FK → `quiz.id` |
| `userid` | `BIGINT` | FK → `user.id` (the student) |
| `attemptid` | `BIGINT` | FK → `quiz_attempts.id` |
| `timecreated` | `BIGINT` | Import timestamp |

**Indexes:** `quizid`, `userid`

This table allows the plugin to:
- Identify which attempts it "owns" (vs. attempts made directly in Moodle).
- Replace an existing attempt on re-import rather than creating a duplicate.

---

## `quiz_paperentry_mappings`

Stores value-substitution rules for a quiz.

| Column | Type | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `id` | `BIGINT` | Primary key |
| `quizid` | `BIGINT` | FK → `quiz.id` |
| `fromvalue` | `VARCHAR(255)` | The shorthand value the grader types |
| `tovalue` | `VARCHAR(255)` | The full Moodle answer text |
| `timecreated` | `BIGINT` | Unix timestamp |

**Indexes:** `quizid`

---

## Entity-relationship diagram

```
quiz (Moodle core)
 ├─< quiz_paperentry_settings   (1 row per quiz)
 ├─< quiz_paperentry_graders    (1 row per grader per quiz)
 ├─< quiz_paperentry_mappings   (N rows per quiz)
 └─< quiz_paperentry_attempts   (1 row per student import per quiz)
       └──> quiz_attempts (Moodle core)

user (Moodle core)
 ├─< quiz_paperentry_graders    (grader assignments)
 └─< quiz_paperentry_attempts   (student import records)
```

---

## Grader CSV files

Grader answer-sheet uploads are **not** stored in any of these tables. They are
stored in the **Moodle File API** under the `quiz_paperentry / submission` file
area. See [Architecture → File API storage](/technical/architecture/#file-api-storage)
for details.
How Paper Entry handles personal data in compliance with GDPR.

import { Aside } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

Paper Entry implements the Moodle Privacy API and is fully compliant with GDPR
requirements. This page describes what personal data is stored, why, and what
happens when a data subject requests export or deletion.

---

## Data inventory

### Student data

| Data item | Location | Reason stored |
|---|---|---|
| User ID | `quiz_paperentry_attempts.userid` | Links import record to the student |
| Quiz attempt | `quiz_attempts.*` (Moodle core table) | The actual graded attempt |

The quiz attempt itself (grades, responses) is stored in **Moodle core tables**
and is managed by Moodle core's Privacy API — Paper Entry only stores a reference
to it.

### Grader data

| Data item | Location | Reason stored |
|---|---|---|
| User ID | `quiz_paperentry_graders.userid` | Records who is assigned as a grader |
| Uploaded CSV file | Moodle File API (`quiz_paperentry / submission`) | The grader's answer sheet |

---

## Privacy metadata declaration

The `get_metadata()` method declares:

```
quiz_paperentry_graders table → userid, quizid, timecreated
quiz_paperentry_attempts table → userid, quizid, attemptid, timecreated
Pluginfile area quiz_paperentry/submission → grader-uploaded CSV files
```

---

## Data export

When a Moodle administrator exports data for a user:

- **As a student:** any `quiz_paperentry_attempts` records for the user are exported,
  along with a note linking to the full quiz attempt data managed by Moodle core.
- **As a grader:** any `quiz_paperentry_graders` assignments are exported, and any
  uploaded CSV file is included via `writer->export_area_files()`.

---

## Data deletion

### Delete all users in a context

Called when a course or activity is deleted. Removes:
- All `quiz_paperentry_graders` rows for the context.
- All `quiz_paperentry_attempts` rows for the context.
- All submission files in the context (`$fs->delete_area_files($contextid, 'quiz_paperentry', 'submission')`).

### Delete data for a specific user

Called when a user exercises their "right to erasure". Removes:
- `quiz_paperentry_graders` rows where `userid = $user->id`.
- `quiz_paperentry_attempts` rows where `userid = $user->id`.
- The user's submission file (`itemid = $user->id`).

<Aside type="caution">
  Deleting a student's paper-entry attempt record does **not** automatically delete
  the quiz attempt in Moodle core — that is managed separately by Moodle's quiz
  Privacy provider. The two deletions happen in the same privacy request but are
  handled by independent providers.
</Aside>

### Bulk deletion (userlist)

The plugin implements `core_userlist_provider` for efficient bulk deletions — for
example when a course is reset or a large cohort of users is anonymised.

---

## File security

Grader submission files are stored via the Moodle File API with the module context
as the parent context. Access to these files is controlled by Moodle's file-serving
layer (`pluginfile.php`), which enforces capability checks before serving the file.

A grader can only download their own submission file. A manager can download all
submission files for a quiz they manage.

<Aside type="note">
  CSV submission files contain student identifiers (email, ID number) entered by
  graders. These are personal data under GDPR and are deleted when a student's
  erasure request is processed — however, the deletion targets the *grader's* file,
  not the student record directly. Ensure your data-retention policy accounts for
  this indirect reference.
</Aside>
Running and writing automated tests for Paper Entry.

import { Aside } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

Paper Entry includes a PHPUnit test suite covering the core import logic, export
utilities, and the Privacy API provider.

---

## PHPUnit

### Running the tests

```bash
# From the Moodle root
vendor/bin/phpunit mod/quiz/report/paperentry/tests/

# Run a single test file
vendor/bin/phpunit mod/quiz/report/paperentry/tests/import_test.php

# Run with coverage (requires Xdebug or PCOV)
vendor/bin/phpunit --coverage-html /tmp/coverage \
    mod/quiz/report/paperentry/tests/
```

<Aside type="tip">
  Run `php admin/tool/phpunit/cli/init.php` once to initialise the PHPUnit
  environment before the first test run.
</Aside>

---

### Test files

| File | Coverage |
|---|---|
| `tests/import_test.php` | Two-pass import, bad-answer abort, unknown user skip, wrong header, re-import replace |
| `tests/export_test.php` | `csv_safe()` injection prevention, question filtering, student exclusion of teachers, duplicate name suffixing |
| `tests/privacy/provider_test.php` | Context discovery for grader & student, users-in-context, delete-all, delete-per-user, bulk delete |

---

### Key test cases

#### `import_test.php`

```php
// Bad answer aborts entire import — zero DB writes
public function test_bad_answer_aborts_entire_import(): void {
    // ... set up quiz with multichoice question ...
    $csv = "email,Q1\nstudent@example.com,INVALID_ANSWER\n";
    $result = $importer->process($tmpfile, $quizid);
    $this->assertFalse($result);
    $this->assertNotEmpty($importer->get_errors());
    $this->assertEquals(0, $DB->count_records('quiz_paperentry_attempts'));
}
```

#### `export_test.php`

```php
// csv_safe prevents formula injection — data provider
public function csv_safe_provider(): array {
    return [
        ['=SUM(A1)', "'=SUM(A1)"],
        ['+cmd',    "'+cmd"],
        ['normal',  'normal'],
    ];
}
#[DataProvider('csv_safe_provider')]
public function test_csv_safe(string $input, string $expected): void {
    $this->assertEquals($expected, export::csv_safe($input));
}
```

#### `privacy/provider_test.php`

```php
// Delete for user removes grader record and submission file
public function test_delete_data_for_user(): void {
    // ... set up grader with submission ...
    provider::delete_data_for_user(new approved_contextlist($grader, 'quiz_paperentry', [$context->id]));
    $this->assertEquals(0, $DB->count_records('quiz_paperentry_graders', ['userid' => $grader->id]));
    $file = $fs->get_file($context->id, 'quiz_paperentry', 'submission', $grader->id, '/', 'submission.csv');
    $this->assertFalse($file);
}
```

---

## Behat (acceptance tests)

Behat tests are not included in the initial release. They can be added to
`tests/behat/` following the standard Moodle Behat structure.

To run any Behat tests tagged `@quiz_paperentry`:

```bash
php admin/tool/behat/cli/run.php --tags=@quiz_paperentry
```

---

## Code style checking

Paper Entry follows the Moodle coding standard. Run the code checker before
submitting a pull request:

```bash
php local/codechecker/cli/check.php mod/quiz/report/paperentry
```

Or with the Moodle PHP CodeSniffer ruleset directly:

```bash
vendor/bin/phpcs --standard=moodle mod/quiz/report/paperentry
```

---

## Continuous integration

The repository includes a `.github/workflows/` directory with a GitHub Actions
workflow that runs:

1. PHPUnit tests against Moodle 5.0 and 5.1
2. PHP CodeSniffer (Moodle standard)
3. `moodle-plugin-ci` linting (JavaScript, Mustache, lang strings)

<Aside type="note">
  The GitHub Actions workflow requires `MOODLE_BRANCH` and `DB` environment
  variables. See `.github/workflows/ci.yml` for the full configuration.
</Aside>
Exact specification of the Paper Entry answer-sheet CSV.

import { Aside } from '@astrojs/starlight/components'; ## File requirements | Property | Requirement | |---|---| | Encoding | **UTF-8** (BOM is tolerated) | | Delimiter | Comma (`,`) | | Line ending | LF or CRLF | | File extension | `.csv` | | Header row | **Required** (first row) | | Maximum rows | No hard limit — limited by PHP `memory_limit` and `max_execution_time` | --- ## Header row The first row must contain column headers. The plugin uses header values to match columns to questions and profile fields. ### Question columns Each question is identified by its **name** (the "Question name" field in the Moodle question editor). Column headers must match question names exactly (case-insensitive, leading/trailing whitespace is ignored). If two questions in the quiz share the same name, the export appends a slot suffix (`_slot2`, `_slot3`, …) to disambiguate: ``` Q1,Q2,Q2_slot3,Q3 ``` ### Profile columns Extra columns (email, ID number, custom fields) use the Moodle profile field shortname: | Column header | Meaning | |---|---| | `email` | Student's Moodle account email | | `idnumber` | Institutional ID number | | `firstname` | First name | | `lastname` | Last name | | `profile_field_class` | Custom profile field with shortname `class` | The plugin uses the first profile column it recognises to identify the student row. Unknown column headers are silently ignored. --- ## Answer values ### Multichoice questions Enter the answer text exactly as it appears in the question options. The match is **case-insensitive** and **strips HTML tags** and leading/trailing whitespace: | Question option | Valid CSV values | |---|---| | `Paris` | `paris`, `PARIS`, `Paris` | | `True` | `true`, `TRUE`, `True` |
Use [Value Substitutions](/configuration/value-substitutions/) to map short notations (e.g. `a`, `b`, `c` or `1`, `2`, `3`) to full answer texts.
### True/False questions True/false questions accept the localised true/false string (e.g. `True` / `False` in English). Value substitutions are the recommended way to let graders enter `T`/`F`. ### Empty cells Leave a cell empty to record "no response" for that question. The student will receive 0 marks for the question. --- ## Example A quiz with questions "Capital" (multichoice: Paris / London / Berlin) and "Island?" (true/false), exported with an `idnumber` column: ```csv idnumber,Capital,Island? S001,Paris,True S002,London,False S003,Berlin,True S004,, ``` Row 4: student `S004` left both questions unanswered. --- ## Validation rules The import performs a **two-pass validation**: 1. **Pass 1** — every row is checked before anything is written to the database. - Unknown student identifier → row is **skipped** (warning, not error). - Unrecognised answer value → **error**; the entire import is aborted. - Missing required profile column → **error**. 2. **Pass 2** — if zero errors, all validated rows are committed to the database. This means you either get a complete successful import or nothing is written at all — there is no partial import state. --- ## Formula-injection protection All values written to exported CSVs are passed through `csv_safe()`, which prefixes any cell that starts with `=`, `+`, `-`, or `@` with a single quote. This prevents Excel and LibreOffice from interpreting cell values as formulas.
This protection applies to the **export** direction only. Graders' uploaded files are read as raw text and never executed, so formula injection from uploads is not a concern.
Correcting a single student's answer without re-uploading a full CSV.

import { Aside } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

The **Edit Answer** panel lets a manager correct one answer for one student directly
in the Moodle interface — no need to modify and re-upload a CSV file.

---

## When to use it

| Situation | Recommendation |
|---|---|
| One student's answer was mis-transcribed | Edit Answer |
| A remark changes a single question for one student | Edit Answer |
| Multiple students need corrections | Re-upload the grader's CSV |
| You want to change many questions at once | Re-upload via Direct Import |

---

## How it works

<Aside type="note">
  Edit Answer modifies an **existing** Moodle quiz attempt that was already committed
  to the gradebook. It is equivalent to opening the attempt in the quiz engine and
  changing the response — grades are recalculated immediately.
</Aside>

### Step-by-step

1. Open the quiz → Paper Entry tab (manager view).
2. Scroll to the **Edit Answer** card.
3. Select a student from the **Student** drop-down. The panel loads their current
   answers.
4. The current answers are shown as a list of question name → current value pairs.
5. Click on the answer you want to change.
6. Type the new value (must match a valid answer option for the question, or a
   configured value substitution).
7. Click **Save**.

The attempt is updated and the grade is recalculated in real time.

---

## Constraints

- The student must have an existing attempt created by Paper Entry (or any Moodle
  quiz attempt).
- The new answer must be a valid option for the question. Invalid values are
  rejected with an error message showing the valid options.
- Edit Answer works on `multichoice` and `truefalse` questions only — the same
  constraint as the CSV import.

---

## Audit trail

Every edit is recorded in Moodle's standard event log:

- Event: `\quiz_paperentry\event\answer_edited`
- Actor: the manager who made the change
- Context: module context of the quiz
- Extra data: student userid, question slot, old value, new value

This means edits are visible in the **Site administration → Reports → Logs** view,
providing a full audit trail for re-mark disputes.
How a grader submits their paper-exam answers in Paper Entry.

import { Aside } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

A **grader** is a teacher who has been assigned to a quiz by a manager. Their job is
to mark the paper answer sheets and upload a filled CSV to Moodle.

---

## Prerequisites

- A manager must have added you as a grader for the quiz (see
  [Manager Workflow → Managing graders](/user-guide/manager/#managing-graders)).
- You need access to the quiz (typically the Teacher or Non-editing teacher role in
  the course).

---

## Step 1 — Open the Paper Entry tab

Navigate to the quiz and click the **Paper Entry** tab. If you are assigned as a
grader you will see the **Submit Your Answers** form. You will *not* see the manager
sections (grader list, comparison table, gradebook import button).

---

## Step 2 — Download the grading sheet

Download the grading sheet yourself using the **Download grading sheet** button on the Paper
Entry tab (visible to anyone with the `quiz/paperentry:view` capability, which the
Teacher role has by default).

The grading sheet has one row per student and one column per question, plus any extra
profile columns the manager configured.

---

## Step 3 — Fill in the CSV from the paper sheets

Once students have sat their paper exam and the answer sheets are collected, open the
grading sheet in a spreadsheet application (Excel, LibreOffice Calc, Numbers) and type
each student's answers as recorded on their paper.

### Answer values

Enter the answer exactly as it appears in the question — typically a letter (`a`,
`b`, `c`, `d`) or a short text value (`True`, `False`).

<Aside type="tip">
  If the manager has set up **value substitutions**, you can use the shorthand values
  they defined (e.g. `1`, `2`, `3` or `T`, `F`). The plugin will translate these
  automatically at import time.
</Aside>

### Leaving an answer blank

Leave the cell empty if a student did not answer a question. The import will record
it as "no response" rather than a wrong answer.

---

## Step 4 — Upload your CSV

1. In the **Submit Your Answers** card, click **Choose file** (or drag your CSV onto
   the upload area).
2. Select your filled CSV file.
3. Click **Submit**.

The plugin validates your file immediately. If any answer does not match a valid
option for a question, the import is **aborted** and a detailed error message is
shown:

```
Row 4: "T" does not match any answer option for question "Capital cities".
Valid options are: "Paris", "London", "Berlin", "Madrid".
```

Fix the answer in your CSV and re-upload.

<Aside type="note">
  Your submission replaces any previous submission. You can re-upload as many times
  as needed until the manager triggers the final import.
</Aside>

---

## Step 5 — Check your conflict status and re-upload if needed

After a successful upload, you will see your own **conflict status table** — a
colour-coded view that shows which answers disagree with other graders,
**without revealing what the other graders entered** (each grader works independently).

- **Green cell** — all graders agree on this answer.
- **Red cell** — your answer differs from another grader's answer for this question.

If you see red cells, review the relevant paper sheets, correct the answers in your
CSV, and re-upload. You can re-upload as many times as needed.

<Aside type="note">
  You do not need to coordinate directly with other graders — just fix the answers
  you believe are wrong based on the paper sheets and re-upload. The manager monitors
  the overall status and will follow up if needed.
</Aside>

---

## What happens after all cells are green?

Once every grader's submission agrees on every answer, the manager sees a fully green
comparison table (with student names visible to them) and clicks **Submit to Gradebook**.
Student grades appear in the quiz gradebook automatically — you don't need to do
anything else.

---

## Troubleshooting

| Symptom | Fix |
|---|---|
| "Submit Your Answers" form not visible | Ask the manager to add you as a grader |
| Upload rejected — bad answer | Check the valid options shown in the error message and correct your CSV |
| Upload rejected — unknown student | The student's identifier in the CSV doesn't match a Moodle account — check the profile column value |
| You can see the manager dashboard | You have the `manage` capability — you are acting as a manager, not a grader |
Everything a manager (course coordinator) can do in Paper Entry.

import { Aside } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

The manager view is available to anyone with the `quiz/paperentry:manage` capability
(typically the **Manager** role or a site administrator). It provides full control
over both import workflows.

---

## The two workflows at a glance

| | Workflow A — Direct import | Workflow B — Multi-grader |
|---|---|---|
| **Who grades** | Manager alone | Independent graders |
| **Comparison** | None | Colour-coded disagreement table |
| **Gating** | Import immediately | Blocked until all graders agree |
| **Best for** | Small classes, single-teacher, emergency | Formal exams, double-marking |
| **UI section used** | Direct Import — Admin Override | Graders + Comparison + Submit |

---

## Dashboard sections

| # | Section | Workflow |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Export Settings | Both |
| 2 | Download / Export | Both |
| 3 | Graders | B only |
| 4 | Grader Submissions | B only |
| 5 | Grader Comparison | B only |
| 6 | Edit Answer | Both |
| 7 | Submit to Gradebook | B only |
| 8 | Direct Import — Admin Override | A only |

---

## Export settings

Use the **Export Settings** card to select which additional student profile columns
appear in the grading sheet alongside the question answer columns.

Available columns include `idnumber`, `email`, `firstname`, `lastname`, and any
custom profile fields defined on the site.

Click **Save settings** after making changes. Settings are stored per quiz.

<Aside type="tip">
  Adding an `idnumber` column makes it easier for graders to identify students —
  they can read the number from a student ID card rather than typing full names.
</Aside>

---

## Workflow A — Direct import

Expand the **Direct Import — Admin Override** card (collapsed by default), choose a
filled CSV, and click **Import**.

- Validates all rows before writing anything (two-pass).
- If any answer is invalid the entire import is aborted with a clear error message
  showing the valid options.
- Re-importing replaces previous Paper Entry attempts — no duplicates.

<Aside type="caution">
  The override import commits immediately to the gradebook — there is no comparison
  step. Use it only when you are confident in the data.
</Aside>

---

## Workflow B — Multi-grader

### Step 1 — Assign graders

In the **Graders** card, type a teacher's name and click **Add grader**. Repeat for
each independent grader (typically 2). Each grader immediately gains access to the
**Submit Your Answers** upload form.

To remove a grader, click **Remove** next to their name. This revokes their access
and deletes their submitted CSV.

### Step 2 — Students sit the exam; graders fill the CSV

Students sit their regular paper exam as normal — they don't interact with Moodle
at this stage. Each grader independently downloads the grading sheet (available to them
via the **Download grading sheet** button on their Paper Entry tab view), fills it in
with the students' answers from the collected paper sheets, and uploads it via
**Submit Your Answers**. Once submitted, their file appears in the **Grader
Submissions** list.

### Step 3 — Monitor the comparison table

Once at least two graders have submitted, the **Grader Comparison** table shows:
- One row per student
- One column per grader (showing every grader's answer side-by-side)
- **Green** = all graders agree | **Red** = disagreement

Graders simultaneously see their own conflict status view — the same colour coding
per student and question, but **without seeing other graders' answers**, so each
grader works independently. They can re-upload at any time until all cells are green.

As manager you monitor the overall status. If disagreements persist, follow up with
the relevant grader offline (check the paper copy, discuss the ambiguous answer).

### Step 4 — Submit to Gradebook

The **Submit to Gradebook** button is enabled only when:
- All assigned graders have submitted, **and**
- Every cell in the comparison table is green

Click it to create graded quiz attempts for all students. Grades appear immediately.

---

## Edit Answer

The **Edit Answer** panel lets you correct one answer for one student directly in
Moodle — no need to modify and re-upload a CSV. Select the student, click the answer
to change, type the new value, and save. The attempt is updated and the grade
recalculated immediately.

Useful in both workflows for post-import corrections (e.g. a remark changes one answer).

---

## Value substitutions

If graders use shorthand, define mappings in the **Value Substitutions** card before
they submit (e.g. `T → True`, `1 → Paris`). See
[Value Substitutions](/configuration/value-substitutions/) for details.
